title: Asuka in ep 9 Quick explanation please
display_title: Asuka in ep 9 Quick explanation please
wikitext:
[]: Asuka in ep 9. Quick explanation please             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Mar 18, 2007 10:58 pm i just read the shin-seiki thread on Ritsuko, and I have another question. shin-seiki wrote: Some might raise the objection "Oh, come on! How could they have something so crucial to the plot as that happen off camera?!" Leaving the likely relevant issue of censorship aside, I should point out that this is not the first time Anno has utilized this elliptical narrative technique: in #09, there is a similar case of where a key part of the narrative is skipped; instead, it is left to the audience to figure out why Asuka's demeanor inexplicably goes from this , to this , seemingly in the blink of an eye. ( Clueless Shinji wonders what's gotten into her, too..)

[]: Re: Asuka in ep 9. Quick explanation please             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 19, 2007 2:03 am slothen wrote: what is Shin-Seiki refering to here?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Mar 19, 2007 8:54 am Which happens directly after that sequence... I figured that was the "skipped material" being reffered to, but I don't the "skipped" part is an explanation for the dramatic change. I feel just Asuka's fickle and schitzotypical nature is all the explanation needed.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Mar 19, 2007 9:21 am cat42 wrote: I feel just Asuka's fickle and schitzotypical nature is all the explanation needed.

[]: Re: Asuka in ep 9. Quick explanation please             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Mar 19, 2007 9:46 am slothen wrote: Due to the dialogue, if a key part of the narrative is skipped, it must be outside the timeline of this scene, unless its a visual part of the narrative that we miss because the camera is on Shinji while Asuka puts on the yellow shirt. So to repeat my earlier question, what exactly is Shin-Seiki refering to?

[]: Re: Asuka in ep 9. Quick explanation please             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 19, 2007 9:50 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: My biggest objection is that Shinji's SDAT never skips a beat in the transition.

[]: Re: Asuka in ep 9. Quick explanation please             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Mar 19, 2007 10:42 am Reichu wrote: It doesn't skip a beat during the #04 train ride sequence, either.

[]: Re: Asuka in ep 9. Quick explanation please             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Mar 19, 2007 10:43 am Mr. Tines wrote: slothen wrote: what is Shin-Seiki refering to here?

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:04 am I'm confused... What exactly is this thread about?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:21 am read the op RAWR. j/k i was reading a thread somewhere else, and i saw this snipet that seemed to be a reference to this scene. However whatever Shin-seiki was talking about was not discussed in that thread, so I'm asking about it here, because it intrigued me.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:44 am I gotta leave for work, but I'll try to address this topic tonight or tomorrow. The point, in brief, is that something Shinji did (or, more to the point, failed to do) provoked the abrupt change of demeanor on Asuka's part in this scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Mar 19, 2007 11:56 am It's been a while since I last watch Evangelion, but basically, if I'm right, you're saying that something is missing from between when Asuka comes out of the shower and Shinji tells her that Misato's gone, and when she's wearing the yellow shirt and shuts the door on Shinji. Basically, you're asking what migth have happened in between. Am I correct so far?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 19, 2007 1:05 pm Timstuff wrote: Basically, you're asking what migth have happened in between. Am I correct so far?

[]: Re: Asuka in ep 9. Quick explanation please             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 19, 2007 1:35 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Of course Reichu, by entering this discussion, you know what you'll have to do next, don't you?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Mar 19, 2007 1:36 pm Mr. Tines wrote: That's my take of the original question -- and the answer is nothing happened. Including no hot-blooded male Eva-pilot-like-herself bursting through the door into Misato's bedroom and proceeding to greet her in Japanese fashion .

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Mar 19, 2007 1:38 pm ....Check the script? But not if it impedes the progress of....the mission.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Mar 19, 2007 1:40 pm I think I get it. What was missing was more explicit in the kissing scene, but here its there too: Blah blah you won't look at me, blah we're all alone and im out of the shower look at my boobies sort of thing. Reminds me also of "Damn him, I hate how he ignores me." So there's no brief timeskip, just like there was no timeskip in the kissing scene, and in both scenes whats missing originally is her train of thought, although its shown briefly in 22, with the "you won't even hold me," is voiced-over while they flashback to the kissing scene. Cool. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 19, 2007 1:43 pm BobBQ wrote: You're saying she wanted that to happen?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Mar 19, 2007 1:50 pm and I suppose threatening him not to come into the room is a way to provoke him into doing just that. I guess by this point Asuka hasn't figured out how Shinji deals with this sort of confrontation. well, actually its definitely best said in the thread linked by Mr. Tines.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 19, 2007 2:13 pm Very lost with just what this thread's about, but just to recap the 22' part of it: "What are YOU doing there?!" "You won't do anything!" "You won't help me!" "You won't even hold me!" And so on and so forth. In terms of why Asuka's mood changed from happy to angry in the scene in episode 9, I'd say the fact that Shinji was oblivous to her flirting, as usual, is what pissed her off.
